title: A Meta AI security researcher said an OpenClaw agent ran amok on her inbox
description: The viral X post from an AI security researcher reads like satire. But it's really a word of warning about what can go wrong when handing tasks to an AI agent.

title: Tesla’s battle with the California Department of Motor Vehicles isn’t over after all
description: Tesla has filed a lawsuit against the California DMV in the ongoing battle around Autopilot.

title: With AI, investor loyalty is (almost) dead: At least a dozen OpenAI VCs now also back Anthropic
description: While some dual investors are understandable, others were more shocking, and signal the disregard of a longstanding ethical conflict-of-interest rule.

title: Ex-Apple team launches Acme Weather, a new take on weather forecasting
description: The team that sold their last app Dark Sky to Apple are back with Acme Weather, which offers alternative forecasts, rainbow and sunset alerts, and more.

title: Anthropic accuses Chinese AI labs of mining Claude as US debates AI chip exports
description: Anthropic accuses DeepSeek, Moonshot, and MiniMax of using 24,000 fake accounts to distill Claude’s AI capabilities, as U.S. officials debate export controls aimed at slowing China’s AI progress.

title: Google’s Cloud AI leads on the three frontiers of model capability
description: AI models are pushing against three frontiers at once: raw intelligence, response time, and a third quality you might call "extensibility."

title: OpenAI calls in the consultants for its enterprise push
description: OpenAI is partnering with four consulting giants in an effort to see more adoption of its OpenAI Frontier AI agent platform.

title: Guide Labs debuts a new kind of interpretable LLM
description: The company open sourced an 8-billion-parameter LLM, Steerling-8B, trained with a new architecture designed to make its actions easily interpretable.

title: Particle’s AI news app listens to podcasts for interesting clips so you you don’t have to
description: AI news app Particle can now pull in key moments from podcasts, letting readers instantly play short, relevant clips alongside related stories.

title: Spotify rolls out AI-powered  Prompted Playlists to the UK and other markets
description: Spotify continues to test its AI-powered “Prompted Playlist” feature, now rolling out the tool to Premium subscribers in the U.K., Ireland, Australia, and Sweden.

title: VPN flaws allowed Chinese hackers to compromise dozens of Ivanti customers, says report
description: Chinese hackers allegedly broke into the network of an Ivanti subsidiary in 2021. The hackers exploited a backdoor in its VPN product, which allowed the hackers to gain access to 119 other unnamed organizations.

title: How AI agents could destroy the economy
description: Citrini Research imagines a report from two years in the future, in which unemployment has doubled and the total value of the stock market has fallen by more than a third.

title: Defense Secretary summons Anthropic’s Amodei over military use of Claude
description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has summoned Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei to the Pentagon for a tense discussion over the military's use of Claude. Hegseth has threatened to designate Anthropic a "supply chain risk."

title: Wispr Flow launches an Android app for AI-powered dictation
description: The app can support translation in over 100 languages and can work across other apps. Wispr Flow said it has done an infrastructure rewrite that makes dictation 30% faster than before.

title: Anthropic accuses DeepSeek and other Chinese firms of using Claude to train their AI
Apple’s newest AirTags are already on sale if you’re looking to upgrade
Does Big Tech actually care about fighting AI slop?
